_provenance.purpose: sumchain-wire 0.2.2 test-only corroboration vectors for two b0 wire encodings that were not previously frozen: a full multi-chunk ObjectCommitmentV1 and a one-slot OutputManifestV1. TEST CORROBORATION ONLY. This is NOT the B0 protocol artifact, NOT closure-golden-vectors.json, and asserts NO semantic protocol change. It does not modify any B0 protocol hash rule.
produced_by: tools/b0-pre-validator (reference frozen b0 encoders: schema::object, schema::manifest, golden::multichunk_buf)
tools/b0-pre-independent (independent frozen b0 encoders: enc module)
_provenance.agreement: Both independent frozen implementations emit these bytes BYTE-FOR-BYTE identically (diff of the two emitters' output is empty).
_provenance.source_base_commit_note: Base tree these vectors were derived from. It is a PRE-PATCH commit and does NOT contain the emitters referenced in produced_by (those are added by the 0.2.2 patch itself); this is the base, not the producing commit.

reproduction_commands.agreement_check: emitter_a stdout == emitter_b stdout, and both equal the *.bytes fields below
reproduction_commands.sha256_check: the b0_corroboration_0_2_2::provenance_sha256_recomputes test recomputes sha256 over the byte fields and asserts it equals the sha256 field below

object_commitment_multichunk.input: ObjectCommitmentV1::commit(ObjectKind::Model, multichunk_buf) with multichunk_buf = vec of length 2*CHUNK+7 where buf[i] = ((i*31 + 7) & 0xff); CHUNK = 1048576

one_slot_output_manifest.input: OutputManifestV1 { slots: [ SlotDescriptorV1 { slot_kind: ResidualStream, slot_index: 7, commitment: ObjectCommitmentV1::commit(ObjectKind::ResidualState, b"g") } ] }
